original_abstract: Abstract Deep learning (DL) is revolutionizing evidence-based decision-making techniques that can be applied across various sectors. Specifically, it possesses the ability to utilize two or more levels of non-linear feature transformation of the given data via representation learning in order to overcome limitations posed by large datasets. As a multidisciplinary field that is still in its nascent phase, articles that survey DL architectures encompassing the full scope of the field are rather limited. Thus, this paper comprehensively reviews the state-of-art DL modelling techniques and provides insights into their advantages and challenges. It was found that many of the models exhibit a highly domain-specific efficiency and could be trained by two or more methods. However, training DL models can be very time-consuming, expensive, and requires huge samples for better accuracy. Since DL is also susceptible to deception and misclassification and tends to get stuck on local minima, improved optimization of parameters is required to create more robust models. Regardless, DL has already been leading to groundbreaking results in the healthcare, education, security, commercial, industrial, as well as government sectors. Some models, like the convolutional neural network (CNN), generative adversarial networks (GAN), recurrent neural network (RNN), recursive neural networks, and autoencoders, are frequently used, while the potential of other models remains widely unexplored. Pertinently, hybrid conventional DL architectures have the capacity to overcome the challenges experienced by conventional models. Considering that capsule architectures may dominate future DL models, this work aimed to compile information for stakeholders involved in the development and use of DL models in the contemporary world.
humanized_abstract: Abstract The rapid development of deep learning (DL) has made the corresponding theories and technologies successfully applied in practice a brand-new decision-making method based on evidence. The characteristic function of DL is the representation learning based on non-linear deep structure of the data, which can achieve strong processing ability through the deep-level non-linear transformation of the data feature at least two times. Although deep learning is still an infant scientific field in the form of interdisciplinary research, the survey papers on deep learning model are few that covers all related works. This paper is aimed at summarizing the development of several new models of DL and also putting forward their corresponding applications and corresponding advantages and disadvantages. The results are: a large number of deep learning models are with high efficiency in the specific field and can be applied by multiple models, while DL training models have such disadvantages as long training time, high cost and big samples needed in order to achieve better test results. In addition, the models are prone to deception, incorrect classification and also get easily into local minima, which needs to be optimized in order to get more robust models. And meanwhile, DL has already made great achievements in the fields of health care, education, security, business and more. The commonly used models are CNN, GAN, RNN and other recursive neural networks and also autoencoders, while the other models have not yet been applied sufficiently. It should be said that hybrid conventional DL models can also solve the problems that the traditional model has failed to cope with, such as the capsule network that is expected to become the main model for DL in the future. Finally, the paper is aimed at providing necessary information for the stakeholders related to design and use of the deep learning models in the era of big data.
